sections.opening: Come sit, dear one, the candle glows and the heart is open. We've reached lesson nineteen on our profound journey through the world of Psalms. After discovering in our previous lesson the wonderful power of Sabbath song prayers, today we turn to understand how Psalms become a bridge between heaven and earth — between heavenly message and daily practice. When you recite Psalms, you're not merely reading ancient words. You're creating a direct connection with the force that created the world, entering into intimate conversation with the sacred. This is the time to discover how Psalms transform from reading into true prayer, from text into spiritual experience that changes the entire course of your day.
sections.theoryA:
How to Pray Psalms Spiritually?

True prayer with Psalms begins with understanding that each chapter is actually a key to opening a different spiritual gate. When you open the book, you're not just reading — you're entering into conversation with the Creator of the world. The tradition teaches us that King David created the Psalms as a universal language that can touch every soul in every condition.

The great secret is that every psalm is built in a way that begins by describing the difficult situation, continues with deepening emotion, and ends with recognition of the Creator's power working in the world. This is no coincidence — it's a spiritual structure that leads the soul from a place of pain or insecurity to a place of wholeness and faith. When you read a psalm, you undergo an inner journey that changes your spiritual state.

The proper way to pray Psalms is to begin with clear intention. Ask yourself: what do I need now? Salvation? Healing? Sustenance? Joy? According to your situation, choose the appropriate chapter. This isn't just mechanical reading — it's a conscious choice with the purpose of entering conversation with the heavenly force.

The tradition teaches that the letters of Psalms have special spiritual power. When you say each word with intention, you're actually creating an energetic vibration that connects with cosmic force. This isn't mere utterance — it's activation of real spiritual power that can change reality.

practice1.instruction: Choose an emotional or physical state you're currently experiencing. Look at the list of chapters and their purposes. Choose one chapter that fits your situation. Read it aloud three times in a row, each time with deeper intention.

sections.theoryB:
How to Connect Psalms to Astrology and the Power of Times?

The wonderful secret that few people know is that Psalms are directly connected to the zodiac signs and cosmic forces. The ancient tradition taught that each month has a special psalm chapter that matches the astrological force ruling at that time. This is no coincidence — it's a perfect spiritual system that connects the power of the stars with the power of the holy word.

For example, in the month of Tishrei (Libra) we recite chapter 81 — a chapter of joy and festivals. Libra's energy focuses on balance and judgment, and chapter 81 speaks about the sanctity of time and the proper relationship to spiritual strength. In the month of Kislev (Sagittarius) we recite chapter 30 — this is the chapter of dedicating the house and returning light. Sagittarius seeks truth and leadership, and this chapter speaks about the gratitude that should come after every salvation.

This connection between Psalms and zodiac signs allows us to work with cosmic force more precisely. When you know which sign rules now and which psalm chapter corresponds to it, you can use the power of time most effectively. It's like catching a wave in the ocean — if you know when it's coming, you can ride it and go far.

The connection to numerology is also very important. The tradition of the Baal Shem Tov teaches that every person should say each day the chapter that corresponds to their age plus one. A thirty-year-old says chapter thirty-one, a twenty-five-year-old says chapter twenty-six. The explanation is that our number of years represents the spiritual correction we've undergone, and the next chapter represents the correction we need to make this year.

practice2.instruction: Calculate your age and open to the corresponding chapter (age +1). Also check which Hebrew month we're in now and what chapter corresponds to the month. Read both chapters and write what connection you feel between them.

sections.deepDive:
The Hidden Virtue of Chapter 119 — Key to Every Locked Door

There is one chapter in Psalms that is like a master key — a key that opens every locked spiritual door. This is chapter 119, the longest chapter in Psalms with 176 verses. What few people know is that this chapter is built according to the order of the Hebrew alphabet, and it has a wonderful virtue for those who know how to use it.

The Chida revealed a wonderful secret: if you have a problem with a specific person, you can solve it by reading the verses in chapter 119 that correspond to the letters of that person's name. For example, if the name is "Sarah," you would read the verses from the section of the letter Shin (verses 161-168), the letter Resh (verses 153-160) and the letter Heh (verses 33-40).

The great secret is that chapter 119 is like a spiritual map of all the forces that exist in creation. Each letter represents a different spiritual force, and each section of eight verses activates that force in a special way. When you read the verses that correspond to your name, you're actually activating your most personal spiritual power.

This works not just for people's names — it works with words too. Having trouble with sustenance? Read the verses for the letters P-A-R-N-A-S-A-H. Difficulty with health? The letters B-R-I-U-T. This power is so strong that certain sages would say the entire chapter 119 every day as a general virtue for everything.

questions.question: According to tradition, which psalm chapter corresponds to the month of Kislev (Sagittarius)?
questions.explanation: Chapter 30 corresponds to the month of Kislev. This is the chapter of dedicating the house and gratitude for healing, which connects to the month of Chanukah and the power of returning light.

questions.question: According to the Baal Shem Tov's tradition, which psalm chapter should a 25-year-old person say?
questions.explanation: A 25-year-old says chapter 26 (age plus 1). This is an ancient tradition that connects a person's age to the spiritual correction they need to make this year.

questions.question: How many verses are in chapter 119?
questions.explanation: Chapter 119 is the longest chapter with 176 verses. It's divided into 22 sections of 8 verses each, according to the order of the Hebrew alphabet.

questions.question: Which psalm chapter did the sages say is a 'key to every locked door'?
questions.explanation: Chapter 119 is called a 'key to every locked door' because of its special structure according to the alphabet and the wonderful virtue it has for solving problems.

questions.question: How do Psalms construct the spiritual journey of the one who prays?
options: Beginning with joy and ending with sadness
Beginning with difficult situation and ending with recognition of the Creator's power
Beginning with emotion and ending with logic
Beginning with difficulty and ending with fear
questions.explanation: Every psalm is built as a spiritual journey: beginning with describing the difficult situation, deepening emotion, and ending with recognition of the Creator's power. This is a structure that leads the soul from a place of pain to a place of wholeness.

closing.summary: Today we discovered how Psalms transform from reading into true prayer. We learned about the special connection between psalm chapters and the zodiac signs of the year, about the tradition of reciting psalms according to age, and about the hidden power of chapter 119 as a key to every locked spiritual door.
closing.wisdom: When you pray Psalms with true intention, you're not just reading words — you're creating a bridge between worlds.
closing.nextPreview: In our next lesson we'll reveal the great secret of therapeutic Psalms — how each chapter can be a specific virtue for healing body and soul.

faq.question: Is it necessary to say Psalms in Hebrew or is translation also possible?
faq.answer: The sages have disagreement about this. The tradition says that the greatest spiritual power is in the original Hebrew, but intention and feeling are more important than language. If you understand better in translation, start there and gradually progress to Hebrew.

faq.question: How much time should be dedicated to reciting Psalms each day?
faq.answer: There's no fixed rule. You can start with the chapter that corresponds to your age (5-10 minutes) and add the chapter of the current month. The goal is quality, not quantity. Better one chapter with intention than ten without concentration.

faq.question: What do you do if you don't remember your exact age or Hebrew birth date?
faq.answer: If you're not sure of your exact age, take the age you think is closest. Regarding the Hebrew month, you can always check calendars or websites. Intention is more important than absolute precision.

faq.question: How do you know which psalm chapter fits a specific situation?
faq.answer: There are traditional guides that detail the virtue of each chapter. But most important is to open the heart and read several chapters — usually you'll feel which chapter speaks to you most strongly in the current situation.

seo.title: How to Pray Psalms: The Complete Spiritual Guide to Heavenly Connection
seo.description: Discover the ancient secrets of Psalm prayer: connection to zodiac signs, age-based virtues, and the hidden power of chapter 119. A practical guide to authentic spiritual practice.
